title: Walk Around
instruction: Stand up and walk around your room or office for at least 1 minute. Swing your arms. Let your body move.

title: Neck & Shoulder Stretch
instruction: Tilt your head to the right, hold 10 seconds. Then left. Then slowly roll your shoulders backward 5 times. Release that tension.

title: Drink Water
instruction: Get up, go fill a glass of water, and drink it slowly. Staying hydrated improves focus and reduces headaches.

title: 20-20-20 Eye Rest
instruction: Look at something at least 20 feet (6m) away for 20 seconds. Blink slowly 10 times. Your eyes have been working hard — give them a break.

title: Wrist & Forearm Stretch
instruction: Extend your right arm, pull fingers back gently with your left hand — hold 15s. Repeat other side. Then make fists and open wide 5 times.

title: Deep Breathing
instruction: Breathe in deeply for 4 counts. Hold for 4 counts. Exhale slowly for 6 counts. Repeat 5 times. This activates your parasympathetic nervous system.

title: 10 Standing Squats
instruction: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower slowly for 3 counts, rise for 2. Do 10 reps. These counter the effects of prolonged sitting.

title: Back & Spine Stretch
instruction: Stand up tall. Clasp hands and reach up to the sky, lean gently to the right for 10s, then left. Then hug your knees one at a time if possible.

title: Make Dhikr
instruction: Leave your screen. Say SubhanAllah 33 times, Alhamdulillah 33 times, Allahu Akbar 34 times. Let your tongue and heart rest in remembrance.

title: Calf Raises
instruction: Stand near a wall for balance. Rise up on your toes, hold 2 seconds, lower slowly. Do 15 reps. Improves circulation in your legs.

title: Look at Something Green
instruction: Go to a window or find a plant. Focus on greenery for 30 seconds. Natural scenes reduce cortisol. If no window is available, close your eyes and visualize.

title: Gratitude Moment
instruction: Step away from your screen. Think of 3 specific blessings from today — no matter how small. Say Alhamdulillah for each one. Gratitude rewires the brain.
